name: Chandrayaan-1
first: India's first lunar mission; confirmed widespread water on the Moon
description: ISRO's first interplanetary spacecraft. The orbiter delivered the Moon Impact Probe to the lunar surface — the first Indian object on another world — and carried 11 instruments from 6 countries. NASA's Moon Mineralogy Mapper (M3) instrument, hosted on Chandrayaan-1, made the discovery that rewrote textbooks: water molecules are widespread across the lunar surface, not just locked in polar shadowed craters. The mission ended abruptly in August 2009 when ISRO lost communication with the spacecraft.

events.label: MIP IMPACT
events.note: Moon Impact Probe targeted hard-landing near south pole.
